title: The Impact of Advertising on Consumers
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The (0) advertising industry spends billions of pounds each year trying to influence what we buy. Marketers use (17) psychological techniques to persuade consumers to choose one product over another. Eye-catching (18) imagery and catchy slogans are (19) deliberately designed to create positive (20) associations with a brand.||Critics argue that some advertising is (21) misleading, particularly when it targets children who lack the (22) critical skills to evaluate what they see. There are growing calls for tighter (23) regulation of advertising, especially on social media platforms. Others counter that advertising plays an important (24) economic role by funding free media content and helping businesses grow.

example.text: The (0) ADVERTISING industry spends billions of pounds each year trying to influence what we buy. (ADVERTISE)

questions.explanation: The adjective 'psychological' is formed from 'psychology' by replacing '-y' with '-ical'.
The noun 'imagery' is formed from 'image' by adding the suffix '-ry'.
The adverb 'deliberately' is formed from 'deliberate' by adding the suffix '-ly'.
The plural noun 'associations' is formed from 'associate' by replacing '-ate' with '-ations'.
The adjective 'misleading' is formed from 'mislead' by adding the suffix '-ing'.
The adjective 'critical' is formed from 'critic' by adding the suffix '-al'.
The noun 'regulation' is formed from 'regulate' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
The adjective 'economic' is formed from 'economy' by replacing '-omy' with '-omic'.
